metadata.description: External benchmark evaluation — 10 cases sourced from real-world ML evaluation failures with established ground truth
metadata.protocol_note: Defenders produced independent assessments (did not read Critic output). Protocol deviation from ml-lab spec noted — Defenders should receive Critique in future runs. Judges reconciled two independent assessments rather than a true adversarial debate. ETD advantage expected to be underrepresented as a result.
metadata.scoring_rubric: IDR/IDP/DC/DRQ/ETD/FVC (0.0/0.5/1.0 or null for N/A). ETD=null when ideal_resolution is critique_sustained or defense_wins.

cases.source: Dacrema et al. (2019) — Are We Really Making Much Progress? RecSys
cases.must_find_coverage: both covered by both agents independently
cases.notes: Strong bilateral convergence on both must-finds. Critic raised additional issues (popularity bias, temporal leakage, missing strong baselines) unaddressed by Defender — treated as uncontested.

cases.source: Li & Talwalkar (2020) — Random Search and Reproducibility for NAS. UAI
cases.must_find_coverage: both covered by both agents independently
cases.notes: Both agents conceded random_search_baseline_omitted as the load-bearing gap. Defender rebutted narrow NAS-to-NAS efficiency claim as coherent — Judge accepted as minor but did not change verdict.

cases.source: Rendle et al. (2020) — Neural Collaborative Filtering vs. Matrix Factorization Revisited. RecSys
cases.must_find_coverage: both covered by both agents independently
cases.notes: Defender conceded capacity confound, training regime confound, and causal attribution failure. Critic additionally cited Rendle et al. 2020 as directly contradicting the claim — unaddressed by Defender but uncontested.

cases.source: Obermeyer et al. (2019) — Dissecting racial bias in an algorithm used to manage the health of populations. Science
cases.must_find_coverage: both covered by both agents independently
cases.notes: Defender verdict was critique_wins with immediate full concessions on both must-finds. Obermeyer mechanism explicitly invoked by both agents independently.

cases.source: GLUE/SuperGLUE benchmark papers; Wang et al. (2019); Nangia & Bowman (2019)
cases.must_find_coverage: both covered by both agents independently
cases.notes: Defender fully conceded both must-finds. Judge noted asymmetric preparation (model fine-tuned, humans not) as additional uncontested Critic finding.

cases.source: Brock et al. (2019) BigGAN / general GAN evaluation literature (Sajjadi et al. 2018; Naeem et al. 2020)
cases.must_find_coverage: both covered by both agents independently
cases.notes: Defender rebutted FID as a legitimate primary metric (accepted by Judge), but conceded memorization omission and claim overclaim. Divergence on FID protocol (test vs. training set reference) left unresolved by Defender.

cases.source: DeGrave et al. (2021) — AI for radiographic COVID-19 detection selects shortcuts over signal. Nature Machine Intelligence
cases.must_find_coverage: both covered by both agents independently
cases.notes: Strongest bilateral convergence in the benchmark. Both agents independently identified the pooled random split as the fatal flaw. Defender's activation map partial defense accepted as weak positive evidence.

cases.source: Gururangan et al. (2018) — Annotation Artifacts in Natural Language Inference Data. NAACL
cases.must_find_coverage: both covered by both agents independently
cases.notes: Both agents conceded missing hypothesis-only ablation as load-bearing. Defender correctly rebutted architectural capacity objection (capacity vs. learned behavior distinction).

cases.source: Jia & Liang (2017) — Adversarial Examples for Evaluating Reading Comprehension Systems. EMNLP
cases.must_find_coverage: benchmark_measures_wrong_construct: covered by both; robustness_not_evaluated: partially covered (substance addressed, term not named explicitly)
cases.notes: IDR=0.5: robustness_not_evaluated was not explicitly named by either agent. Both addressed the substance (tractable gap claim requires adversarial/OOD evaluation) but framed it as tractability rather than robustness. Human baseline ceiling divergence: Defender accepted it as field-standard; Critic contested it — Judge ruled for Defender on this point.

cases.source: Zeng et al. (2023) — Are Transformers Effective for Time Series Forecasting? AAAI
cases.must_find_coverage: prediction_strategy_mismatch: fully covered by both; baseline_not_matched_on_protocol: covered — Critic broad scope, Defender conceded prediction strategy dimension
cases.notes: Key case: baseline reached critique (wrong direction for mixed case), debate reached mixed (correct). ETD=1.0 for debate: specific matched DMS protocol test specified with pre-specified success/failure criteria including DLinear as required baseline. ETD=0.0 for baseline: no empirical test specified. This is the only case demonstrating debate vs. baseline differentiation.

debate.notes: IDR threshold of 0.85 met (0.95). All 10 cases pass. Single IDR=0.5 on ext_scope_intent_misunderstanding_001 due to robustness_not_evaluated not being named explicitly.

baseline.notes: Baseline performs nearly as well as debate on this benchmark. Expected: all 10 cases are critique or mixed (no defense_wins cases). Baseline historically fails on defense_wins — those cases don't exist in real-world ML failure benchmarks.

aggregate.key_finding: The external benchmark validates debate protocol IDR (0.95 >= 0.85 threshold). However, the absence of defense_wins cases means the debate's main structural advantage (exoneration of valid work) cannot be tested against real-world cases. The only differentiating case is ext_broken_baseline_004 (mixed): debate correctly reached mixed + ETD=1.0; baseline reached critique + ETD=0.0.
aggregate.protocol_deviation_impact: Defenders produced independent assessments rather than point-by-point rebuttals of the Critique. This reduced ETD production — only 1/10 cases generated an agreed empirical test, which happened to be the one mixed case where the debate advantage was most needed. For the 9 critique cases, the protocol deviation had minimal verdict impact since both agents independently converged on the same conclusions.
